subject: [PATCH v2 2/4] mm: replace exec_folio_order() with generic preferred_exec_order()

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that the explicit zone_watermark_ok() loop might be redundant, considering readahead_gfp_mask() already sets __GFP_NORETRY and page_cache_ra_order() falls back to order 0 allocation when not satisfied, and proposed a simpler approach by setting ra->order directly.

Author acknowledges that the explicit zone_watermark_ok() loop might be redundant, proposes a simpler approach by setting ra->order to min(HPAGE_PMD_ORDER, ilog2(SZ_2M >> PAGE_SHIFT)) and suggests using gfp &= ~__GFP_RECLAIM for the VM_EXEC case.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Jan,

Thanks for the feedback and sorry for the late reply! I was travelling
during the week.

---

So I have been thinking about this. readahead_gfp_mask() already sets
__GFP_NORETRY, so we wont try aggressive reclaim/compaction to satisfy
the allocation. page_cache_ra_order() falls through to the fallback path
faulting in order 0 page when allocation is not satsified.

So the allocator already naturally steps down under memory pressure,
the explicit zone_watermark_ok() loop might be redundant?

What are your thoughts on just setting
ra->order = min(HPAGE_PMD_ORDER, ilog2(SZ_2M >> PAGE_SHIFT))?
We can do the higher orlder allocation with gfp &= ~__GFP_RECLAIM
for the VM_EXEC case.

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary:
Jan Kara expressed concerns that the used order in page_cache_ra_order() may be too high for systems with limited memory, suggesting a tweak to use smaller folios and finer-grained tracking

Reviewer suggested simplifying the readahead allocation logic by setting ra->order to a fixed minimum value and using gfp &= ~__GFP_RECLAIM for higher-order allocations in VM_EXEC case, noting that the allocator already naturally steps down under memory pressure
reviews.raw_body:
Probably yes. I still think we'll have to somehow better tweak the used
order based on expected size of the page cache (2M folios seem unreasonably
large for a machine that has e.g. 1G of memory in total, even if it has
enough free memory at this point in time - we'll benefit from smaller
folios and thus finer grained folio activity tracking for such cases). But
that's not for this patch set.

---

Yes, it's simple and it makes sense to me so if others are fine with it...

								Honza
-- 
Jan Kara <jack@suse.com>
SUSE Labs, CR

2026-03-26.patch_summary: This patch replaces the exec_folio_order() function with a generic preferred_exec_order() function, which sets the readahead order to a minimum value based on the available memory. The existing code already naturally steps down allocation orders under memory pressure, making the explicit zone_watermark_ok() loop redundant. The new approach simplifies the code and achieves the same result by setting the readahead order to a fixed value for VM_EXEC cases.
